items.title: Nvidia, Alphabet sit out megacap tech bounce as chip stocks sink; global AI sell-off deepens
items.content: Chip stocks sold off Friday on AI infrastructure cost concerns. Intel -3%, Sandisk -10%, Arm -4%, Marvell -5%, Micron -5% after prior day's surge. SMIC tumbled nearly 7%. Magnificent Seven all down at least 8% in June. Apple +3% on Friday after prior day's slide. European chip stocks also fell: ASML -2%, Infineon -4%.

items.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq Post Weekly Losses as Tech Sell-Off Continues
items.content: S&P 500 and Nasdaq posted weekly losses for the week ending June 26 as tech sell-off continued. Earlier in the month Dow hit record close of 51,999.67 on June 16. Market rotating out of tech into cyclicals amid declining oil prices.

items.title: PCE inflation report May 2026: Core inflation 3.4%, highest since Oct 2023
items.content: Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge: headline PCE 4.1% annual rate (highest since April 2023), core PCE 3.4% (highest since Oct 2023). Monthly PCE +0.4%. Energy prices biggest driver due to Iran war. Consumer spending +0.7% beat expectations. Markets see Fed rate hike likely in September.

items.title: May US PCE inflation tops 4%, leaves Fed hike on the table
items.content: May PCE inflation topped 4%, keeping September rate hike firmly in play. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh committed to bringing inflation down. Energy prices surged 4% monthly due to Iran conflict spillover effects.

items.title: US PCE price index expected to rise 0.5% MoM in May 2026
items.content: US PCE price index expected to rise 0.5% month-over-month in May 2026, accelerating from 0.4% in April, as higher energy costs linked to Iran conflict continue to feed through. At June 2026 meeting, Fed held rates and signaled potential hike.

items.title: Hang Seng Index Drops 1.76% to 22,671 as AI Sell-Off Hits One-Year Low
items.content: Hang Seng Index closed Friday June 26 at 22,671, down 1.76%, hitting a one-year low. Driven by global AI-related sell-off. Earlier in the week HSI was at 23,420 (June 24) before sliding. CSI 300 also fell amid broad tech weakness.

items.title: Chinese Stocks in Hong Kong Rebound From Edge of Bear Market
items.content: Hang Seng China Enterprises Index narrowed decline to 0.3% from loss of as much as 2.3%, with insurers like China Life Insurance rebounding. Stocks near bear market territory amid AI sell-off after holiday.

items.title: 2026 Iran War: Iran closed Strait of Hormuz on June 20; renewed ceasefire in Lebanon
items.content: On June 19, Trump announced renewed ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah. On June 20, Iran declared closure of Strait of Hormuz. US conducted freedom of navigation operation through Strait. US and Iran nearing broader peace agreement as of late May. Oil prices dropped to lowest since war began.

items.title: SSE Composite Index Slides 2.26% to 4,027 as Global AI Sell-Off and OpenAI IPO Delay Rattle China Markets
items.content: China SSE Composite Index closed at 4,027 on June 26, down 2.26%. Decline driven by global AI sell-off and OpenAI IPO delay. Index still up ~21% YoY as of June 2026, reflecting PBOC support and earlier AI investment wave.

items.title: Hang Seng rose 0.36% to 23,420; CSI 300 up 0.48% to 4,943
items.content: Hong Kong's Hang Seng index rose 0.36% to 23,420.4 on June 24, while mainland CSI 300 was up 0.48% ending at 4,943.02, amid brief relief from selling pressure.

items.title: Micron tops Q3 earnings estimates, offers better-than-expected outlook and declares dividend
items.content: Micron (MU) announced Q3 earnings beating analysts' expectations on top and bottom lines. However stock fell 6.69% following the report as broader AI sell-off weighed on semiconductor sentiment.

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average closes at record 51,999.67
items.content: Dow rose 328.64 points (+0.64%) to record close of 51,999.67 on June 16. Investors rotated out of chipmakers into cyclical stocks amid declining oil prices. This was the high point before the June sell-off.
